strategy.description:
Startup strategy controlling how initialization failures are handled.

- `blocking`: Initialization runs before DNS resolution starts; errors are logged but Blocky keeps running if possible.
- `failOnError`: Like blocking but Blocky exits with an error if initialization fails.
- `fast`: Blocky serves DNS immediately and runs initialization in the background.

init.description: Initialization strategy controlling when upstream resolvers are tested on startup.

timeout.description: Timeout for upstream DNS connections; a value <= 0 is reset to the default.

groups.description: Named groups of upstream DNS resolvers; the "default" group is required.

strategy.description:
Strategy for selecting which upstream(s) to use per query (parallel_best, random, strict).

- `parallel_best`: Picks 2 random weighted resolvers per query and returns the fastest answer (default).
- `strict`: Queries upstreams in strict order; the next is tried only if the previous fails.
- `random`: Picks one random weighted resolver per query; another is tried on failure.

userAgent.description: HTTP User-Agent header sent when connecting to DoH upstream servers.

maxIdleTimeout.description: Maximum idle duration before the QUIC connection is closed.

keepAlivePeriod.description: Interval at which keep-alive packets are sent to maintain the QUIC connection.

quic.description: QUIC-specific connection settings used when DoQ upstreams are configured.

upstreams.description: Upstream DNS servers and strategy configuration.

connectIPVersion.description:
IP version used for outgoing connections (dual, v4, v6).

- `dual`: Use both IPv4 and IPv6.
- `v4`: Use IPv4 only.
- `v6`: Use IPv6 only.

rewrite.description: Domain rewrite rules applied before resolution; keys are rewritten to their values.

fallbackUpstream.description: If true, the original query is sent upstream when the mapped resolver returns an empty answer.

customTTL.description: TTL for DNS records defined in the mapping section (does not apply to zone file records).

mapping.description: Simple domain-to-IP mappings; multiple IPs per domain separated by commas.

zone.description: DNS zone file content for more complex record definitions (A, AAAA, CNAME, TXT, SRV).

filterUnmappedTypes.description: If true, queries for types not defined for a domain return empty; if false, they are forwarded upstream.

customDNS.description: Custom static DNS mappings and zone definitions.

rewrite.description: Domain rewrite rules applied before resolution; keys are rewritten to their values.

fallbackUpstream.description: If true, the original query is sent upstream when the mapped resolver returns an empty answer.

mapping.description: Domain-to-upstream mappings; queries for each domain are forwarded to its configured resolver(s).

conditional.description: Conditional upstream resolvers for specific domains.

items.description: Source: an http(s) URL, a local file path, or an inline YAML block.

denylists.description: Named groups of block-list sources (URLs, file paths, or inline content).

items.description: Source: an http(s) URL, a local file path, or an inline YAML block.

allowlists.description: Named groups of allow-list sources; entries here take precedence over denylists in the same group.

start.description: Start time in HH:MM format; omit together with end for an all-day schedule.

end.description: End time in HH:MM format; if before start, the window is overnight (e.g. 22:00 to 06:00).

items.description: Day of week: mon, tue, wed, thu, fri, sat, sun.

weekdays.description: Days of the week this schedule is active (mon, tue, wed, thu, fri, sat, sun).

additionalProperties.description: Schedule defines a time-based schedule for blocking rules.

schedules.description: Named time-based schedules that can gate when list groups are active.

listSchedules.description: Maps each list group name to the schedule(s) that control when it is active.

clientGroupsBlock.description: Maps client identifiers (name, IP, CIDR) to the list groups that apply to them.

blockType.description: Response for blocked A/AAAA queries: zeroIP (0.0.0.0/::), nxDomain, or custom IPs; other types get NXDOMAIN.

blockTTL.description: TTL of blocked responses; how long clients cache the block before querying the domain again.

strategy.description:
Startup strategy controlling how initialization failures are handled.

- `blocking`: Initialization runs before DNS resolution starts; errors are logged but Blocky keeps running if possible.
- `failOnError`: Like blocking but Blocky exits with an error if initialization fails.
- `fast`: Blocky serves DNS immediately and runs initialization in the background.

concurrency.description: Maximum number of sources downloaded and processed concurrently (default: 4).

maxErrorsPerSource.description: Maximum parse errors per source before the source is considered invalid; -1 disables the limit.

refreshPeriod.description: How often sources are reloaded; a value of 0 or less disables periodic refresh (default: 4h).

readTimeout.description: Timeout for reading the download response body (default: 20s).

readHeaderTimeout.description: Timeout for reading the download response headers (default: 20s).

writeTimeout.description: Timeout for writing the downloaded file (default: 20s).

attempts.description: Number of download attempts before giving up (default: 3).

cachePath.description:
Directory for the on-disk download cache. When empty (default), downloads are
fully stateless: nothing is written to disk and every source is downloaded in
full on every refresh. When set, blocky uses HTTP conditional requests and
serves unchanged/unavailable sources from this directory.

downloads.description: HTTP(S) download settings for remote sources.

loading.description: Controls how block/allow lists are loaded and periodically refreshed.

items.description: Source: an http(s) URL, a local file path, or an inline YAML block.
Source: an http(s) URL, a local file path, or an inline YAML block.

blocking.description: Blocking configuration with allow/denylists and client groups.

clients.description: Static map of client name to one or more IP addresses for manual client name assignment.

upstream.description: Upstream DNS server used for rDNS client name lookups (typically your router).

singleNameOrder.description: Order of preference when a router returns multiple names for a client (1-based index).

clientLookup.description: Client name lookup configuration for resolving client identifiers.

minTime.description: Minimum TTL for cached entries; if the response TTL is smaller, this value is used instead.

maxTime.description: Maximum TTL for cached entries. If <0, caching is disabled. If 0, the response TTL is used.

cacheTimeNegative.description: TTL for negative responses (NXDOMAIN / empty). Use -1 to disable caching of negative results.

maxItemsCount.description: Maximum number of cache entries (soft limit). 0 means unlimited.

prefetching.description: If true, blocky preloads DNS results for frequently queried names before they expire.

prefetchExpires.description: Time window used to track query frequency for prefetch eligibility.

prefetchThreshold.description: Minimum number of queries within prefetchExpires required to trigger prefetching.

prefetchMaxItemsCount.description: Maximum number of domains tracked for prefetching (soft limit). 0 means unlimited.

exclude.description: Regex list of domains that are never cached.

target.description: Directory for CSV log files, file path for sqlite, or database URL for mysql/postgresql/timescale.

type.description:
Log target type: mysql, postgresql, timescale, sqlite, csv, csv-client, console, or none.

- `console`: Log to console output (used when no type is set).
- `none`: Do not log any queries.
- `mysql`: Log each query to an external MySQL or MariaDB database.
- `postgresql`: Log each query to an external PostgreSQL database.
- `csv`: Log to a CSV file (one per day).
- `csv-client`: Log to a CSV file (one per day and per client).
- `timescale`: Log each query to an external Timescale database.
- `sqlite`: Log each query to a local SQLite database file.

logRetentionDays.description: Delete log entries older than this many days. 0 disables retention cleanup.

creationAttempts.description: Maximum number of attempts to create the query log writer on startup.

creationCooldown.description: Delay between query log writer creation attempts.

fields.description: Which fields to include in log entries; defaults to all available fields.

flushInterval.description:
Interval at which buffered log entries are flushed in bulk to the database
(used by the mysql, postgresql, timescale and sqlite targets).

sudn.description: If true, queries resolved as Special Use Domain Names (SUDN) are not logged.

domains.description:
Domains whose queries are not logged. Each entry is matched against the
query name as an exact domain, a *.wildcard, or a /regex/.

ignore.description: Rules to suppress certain queries from being logged.

enable.description: If true, enables the Prometheus metrics endpoint.

path.description: URL path under which the Prometheus metrics are served.

enable.description: If true, enables in-memory statistics collection and the /api/stats endpoint.

statistics.description: In-memory statistics subsystem (24h window), served at /api/stats.

address.description:
Server address and port (e.g. `localhost:6379`), a unix socket path (e.g. `/var/run/redis/redis.sock`),
or the sentinel master name when sentinel is used. An address starting with `/` is treated as a unix socket.

required.description: If true, blocky will not start when the Redis connection cannot be established.

connectionAttempts.description: Maximum number of connection attempts before giving up.

sentinelUsername.description: Sentinel username (if sentinel authentication is required).

sentinelPassword.description: Sentinel password (if sentinel authentication is required).

sentinelAddresses.description: List of Redis Sentinel host:port addresses; enables sentinel mode when non-empty.

redis.description: Redis configuration for cache and state synchronization between instances.

dns.description: Listen address(es) for DNS over TCP and UDP (default: 53).

http.description: Listen address(es) for HTTP (metrics, REST API, DoH).

https.description: Listen address(es) for HTTPS (metrics, REST API, DoH).

freeBind.description:
Allow binding the DNS and DoT listeners to addresses that are not yet assigned to a network
interface, via the Linux IP_FREEBIND socket option (e.g. for Tailscale/WireGuard/VRRP addresses
brought up after startup). Has no effect on wildcard binds and is ignored, with a warning, on
non-Linux platforms.

ports.description: Listen addresses for DNS, HTTP, HTTPS, and TLS.

minTlsServeVersion.description: Minimum TLS version the DoT and DoH servers use to serve encrypted DNS requests.

certFile.description: Path to the TLS certificate file for DoH and DoT; if empty, a self-signed certificate is generated.

keyFile.description: Path to the TLS key file for DoH and DoT; if empty, a self-signed certificate is generated.

bootstrapDns.description: Bootstrap DNS servers used to resolve DoH/DoT upstream hostnames.

items.description: Source: an http(s) URL, a local file path, or an inline YAML block.

sources.description: Host files to load (e.g. /etc/hosts); supports local paths, URLs, and inline content.

hostsTTL.description: TTL for DNS records resolved from host files.

filterLoopback.description: If true, loopback addresses (127.0.0.0/8 and ::1) from host files are ignored.

strategy.description:
Startup strategy controlling how initialization failures are handled.

- `blocking`: Initialization runs before DNS resolution starts; errors are logged but Blocky keeps running if possible.
- `failOnError`: Like blocking but Blocky exits with an error if initialization fails.
- `fast`: Blocky serves DNS immediately and runs initialization in the background.

concurrency.description: Maximum number of sources downloaded and processed concurrently (default: 4).

maxErrorsPerSource.description: Maximum parse errors per source before the source is considered invalid; -1 disables the limit.

refreshPeriod.description: How often sources are reloaded; a value of 0 or less disables periodic refresh (default: 4h).

readTimeout.description: Timeout for reading the download response body (default: 20s).

readHeaderTimeout.description: Timeout for reading the download response headers (default: 20s).

writeTimeout.description: Timeout for writing the downloaded file (default: 20s).

attempts.description: Number of download attempts before giving up (default: 3).

cachePath.description:
Directory for the on-disk download cache. When empty (default), downloads are
fully stateless: nothing is written to disk and every source is downloaded in
full on every refresh. When set, blocky uses HTTP conditional requests and
serves unchanged/unavailable sources from this directory.

downloads.description: HTTP(S) download settings for remote sources.

loading.description: Controls how host files are loaded and periodically refreshed.

fqdnOnly.description: When enabled, blocky returns NXDOMAIN immediately for non-FQDN queries.

queryTypes.description: DNS query types to drop; matching queries receive an empty answer (e.g. AAAA to block IPv6 lookups).

useAsClient.description: Use the ECS client subnet as the client IP when a full-prefix ECS option is present.

forward.description: Forward the ECS option from the client request to upstream resolvers.

ipv4Mask.description: Subnet mask for IPv4 EDNS Client Subnet; adds ECS option when greater than zero (max 32).

ipv6Mask.description: Subnet mask for IPv6 EDNS Client Subnet; adds ECS option when greater than zero (max 128).

rfc6762-appendixG.description:
These are "recommended for private use" but not mandatory.
If a user wishes to use one, it will most likely be via conditional
upstream or custom DNS, which come before SUDN in the resolver chain.
Thus defaulting to `true` and returning NXDOMAIN here should not conflict.

enable.description: Set to false to completely disable Special Use Domain Name blocking (not recommended for remote upstreams).

specialUseDomains.description: Special Use Domain Names (SUDN) blocking configuration.

enable.description: Enable DNS64 synthesis of AAAA records from A records for IPv6-only clients (RFC 6147).

prefixes.description: IPv6 prefixes used for synthesis; valid lengths are /32, /40, /48, /56, /64, /96 (default: 64:ff9b::/96).

exclusionSet.description: IPv6 prefixes excluded from triggering synthesis. Overrides the default exclusion set; advanced use only.

dns64.description: DNS64 synthesis configuration for IPv6-only clients (RFC 6147).

validate.description: Enable DNSSEC validation of DNS responses.

trustAnchors.description: Custom trust anchors (DNSKEY or DS records); empty uses built-in IANA root trust anchors.

maxChainDepth.description: Maximum domain label depth for chain of trust validation (DoS protection).

cacheExpirationHours.description: How long to cache DNSSEC validation results, in hours.

maxUpstreamQueries.description: DoS protection: max upstream queries per validation

clockSkewToleranceSec.description:
Clock skew tolerance in seconds for signature validation (default: 3600 = 1 hour)
Allows validation to succeed even if system clock is off by this amount.
Matches Unbound/BIND defaults for real-world deployments (VMs, containers, embedded systems).
Per RFC 6781 §4.1.2: Validators should account for clock skew in deployment environments.

enable.description: Enable the HTTP/3 listener on the same addresses as ports.https (requires ports.https to be set).

rate.description: Sustained query rate limit in queries per second per client.

burst.description: Token bucket capacity; queries above this burst are dropped. Defaults to rate × 2.

ipv4Prefix.description: Prefix length used to aggregate IPv4 client addresses into a single bucket.

ipv6Prefix.description: Prefix length used to aggregate IPv6 client addresses into a single bucket.

allowlist.description: CIDRs or IPs that are never rate-limited.

rateLimit.description: Per-client DNS query rate limiting configuration.
